label: Week 21
headline: Sample week. Real SAM.gov pulls begin once the pipeline is live.

awards.summary: Placeholder figures — not real SAM.gov data. Shown only to demonstrate the page layout while the data pipeline is being built.

blog.title: Week one: a soft launch while the pipeline gets wired
body: I spent this week trying to read government award data without forming opinions. That's harder than it sounds. Every line item has a number, an agency, and an NAICS code, and your brain wants to make a story out of it before you've even finished scrolling.

What helped was picking five NAICS codes and refusing to look at anything else. 561110, 561990, 561320, 541611, 493110. Admin services, support, temp help, management consulting, warehousing. Those are the doors I'm walking through. Everything else will still be there in a month when I rotate.

The thing nobody told me: most contracts that show up in award feeds are already closed. The data is backward-looking by design. Which means "watching awards" is really "watching patterns over time." I have one week of pattern. Ask me again in eight.

Closing contracts are the actionable part — what's coming due, who's about to need a new vendor. That's the section I'll be working hardest on in the weeks ahead. Right now I'm just learning the vocabulary.
